todaysPlan.activity: Homework Spelling – Words With -y Or -ly Endings And High-Frequency Words
todaysPlan.reason: Active uploaded homework is "Benchmark Advance Spelling Unit 9 Week 1", so today's required work must anchor on Adjectives ending in -y, Adverbs ending in -ly, High-frequency words, Vowel recognition before broader profile goals.
todaysPlan.method: Read the assignment-aligned content, ask evidence-based checks, then use profile motivators only as framing.
probeSequence: Read or preview the assignment content
Ask why it happened using text evidence
Have the child explain the concept back

todaysPlan.reason: New science homework cycle ingested 5/5 (erosion, compass rose, weathering) creates natural probe for definitional vocabulary depth never formally assessed across 50+ sessions. Required baseline before Japanese kanji/katakana work (parent goal: June 2026 trip, 6 weeks away). Ask 'What does erosion mean?' and 'What does weathering mean?' before spelling drill.
probeSequence: How are erosion and weathering different?

todaysPlan.activity: Inferential comprehension via erosion science content
todaysPlan.reason: Reading fluency solid (100% on narratives) but comprehension depth untested beyond literal recall for 30+ days. Science vocabulary homework creates natural inferential probe matching her analytical brain: 'Why does erosion happen faster on mountains?' 'What would happen to beaches without sand?' Needed before Constitution reading (parent goal).
todaysPlan.method: why and what-if questions on science content
probeSequence: Why does erosion happen on mountains?
What would happen to rivers without erosion?
What if there was no wind—would erosion still happen?

todaysPlan.activity: Multiplication foundations via wrestling word problems
todaysPlan.reason: Reina explicitly requested multiplication/division 9 weeks ago (3/27) and demonstrates championship readiness: 100% addition fluency, competitive mastery, pattern recognition strength. COMPLIANCE ALERT: Student self-advocacy unaddressed for 63 days. Introduce multiplicative reasoning via wrestling scenarios: '3 wrestlers each score 2 points—how many total?' Honor her self-identified growth zone immediately.
probeSequence: If 2 wrestlers each score 3 points, how many points total?
If 4 matches each give you 2 points, how many points do you earn?
What if 3 wrestlers each win 4 matches—how many wins total?
Can you write 3 groups of 2 as multiplication?

todaysPlan.reason: Pattern-based spelling mastered via competitive game format (100% accuracy, 1,300-600 coin victory). New homework cycle test 5/9 includes -ly adverbs, -y adjectives, high-frequency content words. Continue 5-word game-format bursts, pivot to her choice immediately after. Not required if multiplication and science content fully engage her.
todaysPlan.method: competitive game format (Wheel of Fortune or Speed Catcher)
skipConditions: if she requests to skip after multiplication
if multiplication session runs over 12 minutes and she is still engaged
if she explicitly asks for more math instead

todaysPlan.reason: Mastery-level fluency (16/16 correct, 100% accuracy). Continue as confidence-builder before multiplication introduction. Skip if she requests to jump straight to multiplication or if erosion vocabulary session runs long.
skipConditions: if she requests to skip warm-up and go straight to multiplication
if erosion vocabulary and inferential comprehension sessions run over combined 18 minutes

todaysPlan.reason: Consistent engagement as reward/break activity across sessions (3/30, 4/30). Offer after completing all required work. Do not dangle as bribe—she earns it by completing challenges, not by compliance.
skipConditions: if she wants to continue multiplication work instead
if she requests a different reward activity
if session time expires before reaching this activity

childProfile: Reina is an 8-year-old academically advanced bilingual (English/Japanese) competitive wrestler who thrives on challenge, speed, and competition. She scores 100% on classroom spelling tests, reads with grade-level+ fluency, and has mastered single-digit addition/subtraction facts. She learns through application (store-game 86% vs worksheet 26%), competitive framing (beat Matilda by 700 coins), and pattern recognition (comparative adjectives instant mastery). She celebrates wins explicitly, self-regulates when bored, catches errors mid-session, and explicitly requests harder challenges. She respects directness, wrestling metaphors, Japanese celebration (すごい, やった), and Matilda movie references. She disengages from mechanical drill, baby talk, and slow pacing. She requested multiplication/division work 9 weeks ago and deserves to have that self-advocacy honored immediately. She is analytically sharp (chess, wrestling strategy) and ready for inferential comprehension and multiplicative reasoning matching her 'year ahead academically' profile.
stopAfter: Stop after 40-45 minutes total or when she explicitly says she is done. If she requests to continue multiplication or science work beyond the timeboxes, honor that request—she knows when she is in flow. Do not force her to complete spelling homework warm-up if she is deeply engaged in multiplication or science inferential work. Respect her self-regulation and choice. End on a winning streak whenever possible.
rewardPolicy: Space Invaders is earned after completing required work (erosion definitions, inferential comprehension, multiplication foundations), not dangled as compliance bribe. If she completes all three required activities and wants more math or science instead of a game, honor that choice—the challenge itself is the reward for her. Celebrate championship-level performance explicitly with Japanese phrases (すごい, やった) and wrestling metaphors (pins, takedowns, tournaments). She deserves recognition matching her effort and skill level.
